title: Viorna fusca
taxonomy: Viorna fusca is classified within the family Celastraceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the staff vines or bittersweet nightshade family. The genus Viorna is part of the Celastraceae family and contains a single species, Viorna fusca. The species Viorna fusca is native to the tropical regions of Africa. It is a climber or an evergreen shrub that has dark green leaves and small, greenish-yellow flowers.

intro: Viorna fusca, also known as Yellowroot, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its yellow rhizomes and yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in treating conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and skin conditions. Bioactive constituents of Viorna fusca include alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
